splits.canonical_name: Recurring Revenue Command Higher Valuations
members: Recurring revenue models (maintenance contracts, SaaS) command higher valuations than project-based
splits.definition: Recurring revenue business models (maintenance contracts, SaaS) command higher multiples/valuations than project-based models; elevator maintenance higher-margin than construction.

splits.canonical_name: Business Model Selection Rules Differ
members: Choose between scalable (SaaS, software) and service models; each has different financial rules
splits.definition: SaaS and scalable software models operate under different financial rules, unit economics, and growth constraints than service-based models.

splits.canonical_name: Dual Revenue Model Integration
members: Membership + transaction fees create blended model without complexity
splits.definition: Blending membership (recurring) and transaction fees (variable) avoids the complexity tradeoff that pure models require.

definition: Business model choice (recurring, project, service, hybrid) determines unit economics, valuation multiples, and growth ceiling.
rationale: Cluster conflates three distinct ideas: valuation differential, model selection mechanics, and dual-revenue integration. Requires aggressive split.

canonical_name: Fragment Risk from Multi-Avatar Scope Creep
definition: Pursuing multiple customer avatars or product lines simultaneously fragments focus, resources, messaging, and compounds into 4+ mini-businesses within years.
rationale: Original name obscures the root cause (scope creep through multi-avatar pursuit). Rename surfaces the mechanism: fragmentation via layer accumulation.

definition: Outsized returns accrue to entrepreneurs who bet against consensus wisdom, especially those with low agreeableness (natural contrarianism).
rationale: Clean clustering with tight semantic alignment. Centroid name well-chosen. Requires high-agreeableness caveat (personality predictor).

kick_members: Supply of pest control technicians is the bottleneck, not demand for service
rationale: Singleton cluster (size=1) with zero variance. This is a single, domain-specific observation (pest control labor supply), not a generalizable concept. Insufficient support for graph inclusion.

splits.canonical_name: Before/After Visual Proof is Highest-ROI Acquisition
members: Before/after visual proof is the highest-ROI patient acquisition strategy
splits.definition: Before/after imagery is the highest-ROI patient acquisition channel in healthcare/aesthetics verticals.

splits.canonical_name: Before/After Operationalization into Patient Journey
members: Build before/after collection into the patient journey
splits.definition: Systematically embed before/after collection checkpoints into the patient onboarding flow to generate social proof assets.

definition: Before/after visual proof is high-ROI acquisition and requires systematic operationalization into patient workflows.
rationale: Two distinct ideas: (1) effectiveness claim vs (2) implementation practice. Split separates the load-bearing insight from the tactic.

splits.canonical_name: Static + Organic Content Outperforms Video
members: Video underperforms because execution quality varies; static images from camera roll + organic daily posting + repurposed podcast clips maximize ROI
splits.definition: Static content (camera-roll images) + daily organic posting + repurposed podcast clips outperform video for ROI; video execution variance kills performance.

splits.canonical_name: Outreach Drives Predictable Deal Flow; Content is Volatile
members: Content brings sporadic deals initially; over time it snowballs into consistent flow; but outreach drives predictable deal flow month-to-month
splits.definition: Outreach generates month-to-month predictable deal flow; content-driven deals are initially sporadic but compound over time into consistency.

splits.canonical_name: Delivery Work Blocks Marketing Consistency
members: Volatile revenue ($55K good months, low months) indicates delivery work is blocking marketing consistency; need to systemize execution
splits.definition: Revenue volatility (high/low month oscillation) signals owner time spent on delivery blocks consistent marketing execution; requires systemization.

definition: Content channel consistency and delivery work blocking are distinct sources of revenue volatility; outreach outperforms sporadic content initially.
rationale: Three unrelated insights bundled: content format choice, channel mechanics (outreach vs content), and operational constraint. Aggressive split required.

canonical_name: Partnership Equity Must Be Codified Upfront
definition: Partnership equity and legal terms must be written explicitly upfront when stakes are low; handshake deals only work after years of proven trust; retrofit discussions create conflict.
rationale: Original 'Empire Building Delusion' is vague. Rename to surface the actual mechanism: codification timing matters. Handshake deals are a hidden conditional.

aliases: Discipline vs. Liking What You Do

splits.canonical_name: Discipline Only Applies to Disliked Work
members: Discipline is consistency with things you dislike; liking something requires zero discipline
splits.definition: Discipline is the ability to do what you dislike consistently; when you like something, discipline is unnecessary—behavior is intrinsic.

splits.canonical_name: Negotiation Rejection as Learned Skill
members: Must say no without remorse and hear no without loss of enthusiasm; both are learned skills
splits.definition: Saying no (outbound rejection) and hearing no (inbound rejection resilience) are learned skills requiring practice; neither is innate.

definition: Discipline applies only to aversive behaviors. Negotiation rejection (saying/hearing no) is a separate learned skill unrelated to preference-based discipline.
rationale: Two orthogonal concepts forced together: (1) motivation/discipline theory vs (2) negotiation skill training. Split exposes both.

canonical_name: Work Shapes Your Identity More Than Its Outcomes
aliases: Who You Become Is the Outcome
definition: The actual outcome of work is identity/capability development, not external results; judge success by who you're becoming, not results collected.
rationale: Tight semantic alignment across three framings of same insight. Centroid accurate. Clean philosophical claim about locus of value.

aliases: Sales Funnel and Sales Academy Build-Out

splits.canonical_name: Talent Funnel Mirrors Customer Acquisition Funnel
members: Build parallel talent funnel that mirrors your customer acquisition funnel
Scale home services by building parallel funnel for salespeople (app gen → nurture → interview → onboard → retain)
splits.definition: Talent acquisition funnel (sourcing, nurture, screening, onboarding, retention) should mirror customer acquisition funnel structure to achieve parallel scale.

splits.canonical_name: Multi-Stage Talent Screening for Coachability
members: Multi-stage funnel screens for coachability and discipline before hire
splits.definition: Implement multi-round screening to test for coachability and discipline signals before hire; avoid culture mismatches.

splits.canonical_name: Educational Platform as Sales Moat and Demand Driver
members: Educational platform drives demand and integration for the DSO
splits.definition: Offering educational programs (coaching, training) creates switching costs and drives demand integration for the core service business.

splits.canonical_name: Recruiting is Systematic Discipline
members: Recruiting is a discipline requiring multiple interview rounds, contractor vetting, and systematic training
splits.definition: Recruiting requires disciplined multi-round processes: interview rigor, contractor vetting, and systematic onboarding to avoid false positives.

definition: Talent funnel architecture, screening discipline, and educational platform strategy are distinct levers for scaling service businesses.
rationale: Cluster bundles architecture (funnel mirroring), screening mechanics, platform strategy, and process discipline. Four separable ideas; aggressive split.

canonical_name: Patience as Action, Not Emotional State
definition: Patience is action taken despite impatience (figuring out productive interim steps), not an emotional state; without defining the interim action, patience is an abstraction.
rationale: Two framings of the same insight about patience-as-skill. Clean semantic alignment. Centroid well-positioned.

canonical_name: Email Cadence for Long-Term List Health

splits.canonical_name: 2x Weekly Email with Rotated Content Themes Drives Retention
members: 2x weekly email commitment rotating before/after transformations, cool projects, and FAQ; long-term list building for repeat purchases
splits.definition: 2x weekly email cadence with rotating content (transformations, projects, FAQ) builds long-term list health and repeat purchase behavior.

splits.canonical_name: Seasonal Marketing Themes Create Year-Round Campaign Structure
members: Two major seasonal cycles per year enable year-round campaigns; align all channels on single theme per 2-month block
splits.definition: Organize marketing around two major seasonal cycles per year (2-month theme blocks); align all channels on unified theme to multiply reach and memorability.

splits.canonical_name: Email Reactivation via Monthly Themes and Split Testing
members: Monthly email themes (savings, allergies, case studies) with split-tested subject lines drive 20-30% revenue lift
splits.definition: Monthly theme-driven emails (topical angles: savings, health, case studies) with subject-line split testing drive 20-30% revenue uplift in reactivation campaigns.

splits.canonical_name: Pre-Tested Content Repurposing Maximizes Long-Form ROI
members: Structure long-form as 20 shorts sewn together on one theme; use pre-tested material (top tweets)
A print-on-demand educator achieved 2% churn by providing weekly lists of trending products and logos
splits.definition: Long-form content is 20 repurposed shorts (top tweets, verified high-engagement pieces) sewn into one theme; reduces production friction and leverages existing proof.

definition: Email retention strategy combines cadence, seasonal structure, monthly themes, and content repurposing to achieve both engagement and list longevity.
rationale: Four distinct levers: (1) cadence/rotation, (2) seasonal macro-structure, (3) reactivation mechanics, (4) content production efficiency. Separate for clarity.

canonical_name: Owner Time Ceiling Blocks Operational Scaling

splits.canonical_name: Owner Time Consumed by Delivery Blocks Scaling
members: Problem is not money ($700K profit available) but owner's time; 80% clinical work leaves no room to scale operations
Scaling from 1 to 5+ locations requires owner business time; can't be done if owner is 80% clinical
splits.definition: Revenue availability does not constrain scaling; owner time does. If owner dedicates 80%+ to delivery, zero bandwidth remains for operations management and expansion.

splits.canonical_name: Hiring Physician Is Entry Ticket to Multi-Location Scaling
members: Hiring the physician is not optional—it's the entry ticket to scaling; otherwise all growth bounces off time ceiling
splits.definition: In healthcare/professional-services, hiring a peer professional (co-physician, co-provider) to handle delivery is prerequisite for scaling beyond 1-2 locations; no alternative exists.

definition: Multi-location healthcare scaling is bottlenecked by owner delivery time, not capital; requires hiring peer professional to free owner for operations/expansion.
rationale: Two ideas: (1) constraint recognition (time > money) and (2) solution (hire peer). Split exposes both the diagnosis and the remedy.

canonical_name: AI as Leverage Multiplier, Not Business Model Shift
definition: AI changes execution leverage and tools, not business fundamentals; humans + superior tech always outcompete humans + inferior tech (historical law of technology adoption).
rationale: Original name obscures the insight. Rename to 'leverage multiplier' surfaces the actual claim: AI is a force multiplier, not a paradigm shift. Clusters the fundamental law of tech adoption.

kick_members: Rented room for $400/month, then saved $400/month by moving to gym floor
Monthly housing cost: $300-400 when splitting one bedroom in six-bedroom house
rationale: Singleton facts (two variations of same anecdote: living on <$400/month, gym floor), not a generalizable claim or framework. Illustrative detail only, lacks load-bearing insight about motivation mechanics.
